mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents through standardized communication protocols. It simplifies the deployment complexity and enhances security for agent-based applications. Ideal for organizations looking to streamline their AI agent operations while ensuring security and scalability.

steps.title: Add Kagenti Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Kagenti Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install Kagenti using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the official Kagenti Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Kagenti CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required by Kagenti to manage its resources effectively.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/v0.5.1/deploy/crds/kagenti.io_kagent_crd.yaml

```

> This command applies the CRD for Kagenti.

steps.title: Install Kagenti using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti application in the specified namespace using Helm.

```bash

helm install kagenti kagenti/kagenti --namespace kagenti --create-namespace --version 0.5.1

```

> This command installs Kagenti in the 'kagenti' namespace.

steps.title: Create an Example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource (CR) to demonstrate how to deploy an AI agent using Kagenti.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kagenti.io/v1
kind: KAgent
metadata:
  name: example-agent
spec:
  image: kagenti/agent:latest
  replicas: 1
EOF

```

> This command creates a sample agent resource.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kagenti.io/v1
kind: KAgent
metadata:
  name: example-agent
spec:
  image: kagenti/agent:latest
  replicas: 1
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Installation
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Kagenti installation and ensures that the agent is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kagenti

```

> This command lists all pods in the 'kagenti' namespace to verify that the agent is running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kagenti installation and its resources. Be aware that this will delete all associated data and resources.

```bash

helm uninstall kagenti --namespace kagenti

kubectl delete namespace kagenti

```

> This command uninstalls the Kagenti release.
> This command deletes the 'kagenti' namespace and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure that you back up any important data. This step will upgrade Kagenti to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kagenti -o json | jq '.items[] | {name: .metadata.name, status: .status.phase}'

helm upgrade kagenti kagenti/kagenti --namespace kagenti --version 0.5.1

kubectl get pods -n kagenti

```

> Check the status of the pods before upgrading.
> This command upgrades Kagenti to the specified version.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n kagenti -o json | jq '.items[] | {name: .metadata.name, status: .status.phase}'

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'KAgent not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified KAgent resource does not exist in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the KAgent resource is created correctly and check the namespace.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image cannot be pulled from the registry.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and ensure that the image is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application inside the pod is crashing due to misconfiguration or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kagenti' to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes API server is unreachable, possibly due to network issues.

**Fix:**
Check your kubeconfig and network connectivity to the cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new agent types and improved security features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Kagenti agent pod running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n kagenti'.

metadata:

  replicas: 1
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required resources.
